title: US rates cut before September?
description: Combines central bank language, inflation prints, and labor-market headlines into one live probability.
settlementRule: Resolves YES if the Federal Reserve lowers its target rate before the September 2026 meeting concludes.
thesis: Softening activity data and a slower hiring pulse have shifted the market from inflation anxiety toward growth protection.
keyDrivers: Regional factory data is rolling over faster than consensus expected.
Fed speakers are signaling more concern about restrictive policy staying in place too long.
A benign inflation print would likely accelerate repricing across rate-sensitive assets.

timeline.title: Factory reports miss in two regions
timeline.detail: Growth-sensitive contracts weaken and front-end rate cuts move higher.

timeline.title: Governor remarks turn more balanced
timeline.detail: Language shifts from inflation persistence to downside growth risks.

timeline.title: Short-end repricing accelerates
timeline.detail: Probability jumps as traders line up around an earlier easing window.

title: Will OpenAI ship GPT-6 this year?
description: Tracks executive comments, model leaks, hiring signals, and launch-adjacent reporting across the AI beat.
settlementRule: Resolves YES if OpenAI publicly launches a product branded GPT-6 before the end of 2026.
thesis: Supply-chain commentary and talent signals suggest the organization is preparing for a major model cycle rather than incremental iteration only.
keyDrivers: Inference-oriented chip demand is being prioritized across the supply chain.
Hiring patterns imply heavier post-training and deployment operations.
Ecosystem chatter has shifted from capability rumors to launch-timing speculation.

timeline.title: Supplier guidance tilts to inference
timeline.detail: The mix shift implies large-scale serving demand instead of research-only usage.

timeline.title: Hiring expands in evaluation and launch roles
timeline.detail: Team composition begins to resemble a release program rather than exploratory research.

timeline.title: Market reprices after partner commentary
timeline.detail: Probability reaches the high-50s as launch expectations spread.

title: Will oil close above $95 this quarter?
description: Links geopolitical news, supply announcements, and inventory releases to a continuously updated market view.
settlementRule: Resolves YES if front-month Brent crude settles above $95 at any official daily close before quarter end.
thesis: The geopolitical bid remains real, but fresh producer messaging has reduced the odds of an immediate supply squeeze.
keyDrivers: OPEC communication has turned less supportive for a rapid spike scenario.
Inventory draws remain constructive but not panic-inducing.
Risk premium can return quickly if shipping disruptions worsen.

timeline.title: Shipping disruption raises tail risk
timeline.detail: Probability jumps as traders hedge the upside energy scenario.

timeline.title: OPEC officials cool speculative fears
timeline.detail: The path to $95 starts to look less immediate after supply comments.

timeline.title: Inventory release confirms softer setup
timeline.detail: The market slips further while retaining a geopolitical tail.
